rationale: clone_end_point's spec ensures only cloned_ep@ == ep@ (Seq-view equality on id), but det_clone_end_point_equal(r1, r2) requires r1.id == r2.id (Vec<u8> equality). Verus's Vec<T> has uninterpreted View and no axiom lifting Seq-view equality to Vec equality (confirmed locally: `requires v1@ == v2@ ensures v1 == v2` fails for Vec<u8>; even =~= fails). The implication (r1@ == ep@ && r2@ == ep@) ==> r1.id == r2.id is therefore unprovable for arbitrary r1, r2, ep without an extensionality axiom for Vec or strengthening clone_end_point's postcondition. opaque_ok=False forbids broadening tactics.
